title: Password Reset Volume Trending
dataSources: AD Security log `EventCode` 4724 (password reset attempt); `index=okta` `sourcetype=Okta:system` password reset events; ITSM `category=password` incidents
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`), Okta Add-on for Splunk, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow or your ITSM (optional)
spl:
(index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4724 earliest=-90d@d)
 OR (index=okta sourcetype=Okta:system EVENT_TYPE=user.account.reset_password earliest=-90d@d)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count as reset_volume by _time
| sort _time
| trendline sma7(reset_volume) as reset_sma7
| predict reset_volume as reset_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=7
description: Sudden increases in password resets‚ÄĒself-service or helpdesk‚ÄĒoften align with phishing waves, credential stuffing after a breach, or attacker-driven resets. A ninety-day trend with a moving average makes campaign-scale activity visible before individual tickets pile up.
value: Sudden increases in password resets‚ÄĒself-service or helpdesk‚ÄĒoften align with phishing waves, credential stuffing after a breach, or attacker-driven resets. A ninety-day trend with a moving average makes campaign-scale activity visible before individual tickets pile up.
implementation: Normalize multiple sources into one panel or use `eval source_system` before `stats`. Exclude routine bulk resets from known automation via a lookup of service accounts or change windows. When the SMA breaches a static or adaptive threshold, open a phishing hunt and check MFA and impossible-travel dashboards. Add helpdesk ticket volume from ITSM if self-service is low but calls spike.
visualization: Column or line chart of daily reset_volume with SMA; optional forecast ribbon.
knownFalsePositives: Bulk password expirations, helpdesk reset campaigns, and seasonal workforce churn can spike volume without incident ‚ÄĒ baseline seasonally.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`), Okta Add-on for Splunk, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow or your ITSM (optional).
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: AD Security log `EventCode` 4724 (password reset attempt); `index=okta` `sourcetype=Okta:system` password reset events; ITSM `category=password` incidents.
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
Normalize multiple sources into one panel or use `eval source_system` before `stats`. Exclude routine bulk resets from known automation via a lookup of service accounts or change windows. When the SMA breaches a static or adaptive threshold, open a phishing hunt and check MFA and impossible-travel dashboards. Add helpdesk ticket volume from ITSM if self-service is low but calls spike.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4724 earliest=-90d@d)
 OR (index=okta sourcetype=Okta:system EVENT_TYPE=user.account.reset_password earliest=-90d@d)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count as reset_volume by _time
| sort _time
| trendline sma7(reset_volume) as reset_sma7
| predict reset_volume as reset_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=7
```

Understanding this SPL

**Password Reset Volume Trending** ‚ÄĒ Sudden increases in password resets‚ÄĒself-service or helpdesk‚ÄĒoften align with phishing waves, credential stuffing after a breach, or attacker-driven resets. A ninety-day trend with a moving average makes campaign-scale activity visible before individual tickets pile up.

Documented **Data sources**: AD Security log `EventCode` 4724 (password reset attempt); `index=okta` `sourcetype=Okta:system` password reset events; ITSM `category=password` incidents. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`), Okta Add-on for Splunk, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow or your ITSM (optional). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚ÄĒrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wineventlog, okta; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security, Okta:system. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scopes the data: index=wineventlog, index=okta, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚ÄĘ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚ÄĘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚ÄĘ Orders rows with `sort` ‚ÄĒ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚ÄĘ Pipeline stage (see **Password Reset Volume Trending**): trendline sma7(reset_volume) as reset_sma7
‚ÄĘ Pipeline stage (see **Password Reset Volume Trending**): predict reset_volume as reset_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=7


Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Compare Splunk results with the Okta admin console and System Log for the same users, outcomes, and time window, then adjust thresholds to normal org traffic.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column or line chart of daily reset_volume with SMA; optional forecast ribbon.
grandmaExplanation: We track how sign-in and identity activity change over time so we can spot surges, drift, and seasonal norms without guessing.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw directory, IdP, PAM, or device feeds.
